mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, consumer spending power contracts sharply, reducing corporate revenue growth forecasts and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and capital expenditure expansion, which amplifies corporate earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 40 percent over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which validates earnings upgrade cycles and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with accelerating inflation indicates capacity constraints and margin compression, which forces equity valuation multiples to contract and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and CPIAUCSL yields positive forward returns in over 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding labor demand and rising household income, which directly boosts consumer spending forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of monthly observations over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers increase sufficiently to dampen investment and spending, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 50 percent of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5 percent.
